model_narrative: We plan raw-material purchasing and product outsourcing for a refinery over a short horizon under uncertain demand, where demand is described by a tree of scenario nodes each with its own probability. In each period we decide how much of each raw material to buy, and for each product, period, and scenario node we decide how much of that product to outsource to cover any shortfall. The objective is to minimize the total cost, which is the cost of the raw materials purchased plus the probability-weighted expected cost of outsourcing across all scenario nodes.

sets.name: i
sets.doc: the raw materials that can be purchased

sets.name: j
sets.doc: the finished products that must meet demand

sets.name: t
sets.doc: the time periods of the planning horizon

sets.name: n
sets.doc: the scenario-tree nodes, each a possible realization of demand

sets.name: tn
sets.doc: the valid (time period, scenario node) pairs of the scenario tree; a node belongs to exactly one period, so each pair maps a node to the period in which it occurs

params.name: c
params.doc: the present purchase cost per unit of each raw material, in dollars per unit

params.name: a
params.doc: the yield of each product per unit of each raw material, i.e. units of product obtained per unit of raw material consumed

params.name: f
params.doc: the cost of outsourcing one unit of a product in a given period, in dollars per unit

params.name: b
params.doc: the inventory capacity, the maximum total amount of raw material that may be purchased over the whole horizon, in units

params.name: prob
params.doc: the probability of each scenario node occurring

params.name: dem
params.doc: the demand for each product at each scenario node, in units

vars.name: x
vars.doc: the amount of each raw material purchased for use in each period

vars.name: y
vars.doc: the amount of each product outsourced for a given product, period, and scenario node

vars.name: cost
vars.doc: the total cost, comprising raw-material purchases plus the expected outsourcing cost

description: Define the total cost. The total cost equals the cost of all raw materials purchased across every material and period, plus the expected cost of outsourcing, which weights each outsourcing decision by the cost of outsourcing that product in that period and by the probability of the scenario node it belongs to, summed over all products, periods, and scenario nodes. Set the total cost variable equal to this sum.
expected_pyomo:
def objdef_rule(model):
    purchase = sum(model.c[i] * model.x[i, t] for i in model.i for t in model.t)
    outsource = sum(model.prob[n] * model.f[j, t] * model.y[j, t, n] for j in model.j for (t, n) in model.tn)
    return model.cost == purchase + outsource
model.objdef = Constraint(rule=objdef_rule)

description: There is a limit on how much raw material can be purchased over the horizon. The total amount of raw material purchased, summed over all materials and all periods, must not exceed the inventory capacity.

description: Each product's demand must be met in every period and scenario. For each product and each valid pairing of a period with a scenario node, the amount of that product produced from the raw materials purchased in that period, plus the amount of that product outsourced for that product, period, and node, must be at least the demand for that product at that node.

model_narrative: We plan raw-material purchasing and product outsourcing for a refinery over a short horizon under uncertain demand, where demand is described by a tree of scenario nodes each with its own probability. In each period we decide how much of each raw material to buy, and for each product, period, and scenario node we decide how much of that product to outsource to cover any shortfall. The objective is to minimize the total cost, which is the cost of the raw materials purchased plus the probability-weighted expected cost of outsourcing across all scenario nodes.

sets.name: tn
sets.doc: the valid (time period, scenario node) pairs of the scenario tree; a node belongs to exactly one period, so each pair maps a node to the period in which it occurs

params.name: c
params.doc: the present purchase cost per unit of each raw material, in dollars per unit

params.name: a

vars.name: x
vars.doc: the amount of each raw material purchased for use in each period

vars.name: y
vars.doc: the amount of each product outsourced for a given product, period, and scenario node

vars.name: cost
vars.doc: the total cost, comprising raw-material purchases plus the expected outsourcing cost

description: To build the complete model, enforce the following relationships in order. First, set the total cost equal to the cost of all raw materials purchased across every material and period plus the expected outsourcing cost, where each outsourcing decision is weighted by the cost of outsourcing that product in that period and by the probability of its scenario node, summed over all products, periods, and nodes. Second, require that the total amount of raw material purchased over all materials and periods not exceed the inventory capacity. Finally, require that for each product and each valid pairing of a period with a scenario node, the amount of that product produced from the raw materials bought in that period plus the amount outsourced for that product, period, and node be at least the demand for that product at that node.
expected_pyomo:
def objdef_rule(model):
    purchase = sum(model.c[i] * model.x[i, t] for i in model.i for t in model.t)
    outsource = sum(model.prob[n] * model.f[j, t] * model.y[j, t, n] for j in model.j for (t, n) in model.tn)
    return model.cost == purchase + outsource
model.objdef = Constraint(rule=objdef_rule)
def bal_rule(model):
    return sum(model.x[i, t] for i in model.i for t in model.t) <= model.b
model.bal = Constraint(rule=bal_rule)
def dembal_rule(model, j, t, n):
    return sum(model.a[j, i] * model.x[i, t] for i in model.i) + model.y[j, t, n] >= model.dem[n, j]
model.dembal = Constraint(model.j, model.tn, rule=dembal_rule)
